body:
status=open
reason=Bash tool is completely unavailable in this execution worktree — every invocation (even `echo test`, with dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true) fails with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-5a385984-20260708T210302-28d84357'`. The host's /tmp filesystem is full, so the harness cannot even create its own scratch directory for the Bash tool. File-based tools (Read/Write/Edit) still work — confirmed by reading cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go and writing this file — but there is no way to run `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/...`, `lefthook run pre-commit`, or any `git` command to stage/verify/commit a fix. This is an environment-level disk-space blocker on the executing machine (outside repo scope, not something fixable via a code change), not a rejection of the bead's proposed fix. Retry once /tmp has free space again. </content>
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes
rationale: status: open
reason: Bash tool is completely unavailable in this execution worktree — every invocation (even `echo test`, with dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true) fails with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-5a385984-20260708T210302-28d84357'`. The host's /tmp filesystem is full, so the harness cannot even create its own scratch directory for the Bash tool. File-based tools (Read/Write/Edit) still work — confirmed by reading cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go and writing this file — but there is no way to run `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/...`, `lefthook run pre-commit`, or any `git` command to stage/verify/commit a fix. This is an environment-level disk-space blocker on the executing machine (outside repo scope, not something fixable via a code change), not a rejection of the bead's proposed fix. Retry once /tmp has free space again.
</content>

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"system_unready: Description carries PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE with concrete file:line evidence (cli/cmd/work_provider_orphan_linux_test.go:60-140, cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go:44-79, cli/cmd/doctor.go:407), names the failing test (TestWork_WatchKillingWorkerReapsProviderChildWithin5s), states a concrete PROPOSED FIX, and preserves NON-SCOPE constraints. AC is a verifiable go test + lefthook pair. The single prior attempt failed on an executing-machine ENOSPC/Bash-unavailable environment fault, not a bead-content defect, so it does not indicate the bead itself is unclear. {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"system_unready: Description gives explicit file:line root cause (cli/cmd/work_provider_orphan_linux_test.go:60-140, cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go:44-79, cli/cmd/doctor.go:407), names the failing test (TestWork_WatchKillingWorkerReapsProviderChildWithin5s), states a concrete proposed fix direction (isolate provider-launch/shim exec path from project runtime preflight/doctor diagnostics while preserving the production warning), and lists non-scope constraints inherited from the parent. Acceptance is numbered and maps to a runnable test/lint command. The two prior_attempts (exit status 143, and no_changes due to executing-machine /tmp ENOSPC) are transient execution-environment failures, not evidence the bead itself is unclear or unexecutable, so they do not block readiness.","fingerprint":"1c42fa5c195d413edd88648d6f89f082fb166c67b755958a8023ae20d7d5f0f0","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"system_unready","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"1c42fa5c195d413edd88648d6f89f082fb166c67b755958a8023ae20d7d5f0f0"}

status=open
reason=host disk exhaustion (ENOSPC) makes the Bash tool completely non-functional in this execution worktree — every invocation, including a bare `echo test`, fails while the tool tries to mkdir its own working directory under /tmp/claude-501/.... This means no shell command can run: no `go test`, no `lefthook run pre-commit`, no `git add`/`git commit`. I was able to Read source files (cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go) via the Read tool, but any code change would be unverifiable and uncommittable in this state, and per the reliability principles I must not commit unverified/red code. This is an external, host-level blocker (disk space exhaustion on the machine running this worktree, plausibly from concurrent worktrees/executions per prior notes on this box), not a defect in the bead's target code or scope. Retrying once disk space is reclaimed on this host, or dispatching on a healthy worker, should allow the fix to proceed normally.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes
rationale: status: open
reason: host disk exhaustion (ENOSPC) makes the Bash tool completely non-functional in this execution worktree — every invocation, including a bare `echo test`, fails while the tool tries to mkdir its own working directory under /tmp/claude-501/.... This means no shell command can run: no `go test`, no `lefthook run pre-commit`, no `git add`/`git commit`. I was able to Read source files (cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go) via the Read tool, but any code change would be unverifiable and uncommittable in this state, and per the reliability principles I must not commit unverified/red code.

This is an external, host-level blocker (disk space exhaustion on the machine running this worktree, plausibly from concurrent worktrees/executions per prior notes on this box), not a defect in the bead's target code or scope. Retrying once disk space is reclaimed on this host, or dispatching on a healthy worker, should allow the fix to proceed normally.

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"system_unready: Description carries PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE/PROPOSED FIX/NON-SCOPE structure with concrete file:line evidence (project_runtime_preflight.go:44-79, doctor.go:407, work_provider_orphan_linux_test.go:60-140) and names the specific failing test in prose. AC gives a runnable go test scope plus the lefthook gate, labels carry area/kind/priority, parent is explicit. The only real weakness is that this 'part 3 of 3' child carries the exact same root-cause/fix prose as its siblings with no distinguishing slice of work, and the AC does not pin the specific Test* function with -run. Neither defect prevents an implementation attempt: the described fix (isolate the provider-launch shim from project runtime preflight/doctor diagnostics) is a single concrete code change an agent can make and verify against the stated AC. The three prior_attempts all failed on host-level environment blockers (ENOSPC disk exhaustion, exit 143) rather than bead-content defects, so they do not count against this bead's own readiness.","fingerprint":"b0d81eb9966fb493c8c17bd4709f6681341d769cf4ae468de7e7f630d3e72895","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"system_unready","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"b0d81eb9966fb493c8c17bd4709f6681341d769cf4ae468de7e7f630d3e72895"}

status=blocked
reason=Host environment has no free space in /tmp — every Bash tool invocation (even `echo hi`, `true`) fails with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-5a385984-20260711T023811-73efa777'`. The Bash tool cannot create its own sandbox working directory, so no shell command runs at all in this worktree — not `go build`, `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/...`, `git`, nor `lefthook run pre-commit`. Read/Edit/Write tools work fine (no /tmp dependency), so this is not a code-level problem in the target files (cli/cmd/work_provider_orphan_linux_test.go, cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go, cli/cmd/doctor.go) — it is an external host resource exhaustion blocker. Retried the trivial Bash call three times with identical failure; this is not a transient blip. Needs operator intervention to free disk/inodes on the host (or reclaim /tmp) before this bead's AC (go test + lefthook) can be executed and verified.
suggested_action=recheck the external blocker and move status to open when cleared

no_changes

The Bash tool cannot create its own sandbox working directory, so no shell command runs at all in this worktree — not `go build`, `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/...`, `git`, nor `lefthook run pre-commit`. Read/Edit/Write tools work fine (no /tmp dependency), so this is not a code-level problem in the target files (cli/cmd/work_provider_orphan_linux_test.go, cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go, cli/cmd/doctor.go) — it is an external host resource exhaustion blocker. Retried the trivial Bash call three times with identical failure; this is not a transient blip. Needs operator intervention to free disk/inodes on the host (or reclaim /tmp) before this bead's AC (go test + lefthook) can be executed and verified.
